steps.title: Identify Text 2's core critique
steps.content: The key signal: Text 2 states that scholars' focus on “experimental versus conventional poetic forms” has been “inhibiting inquiry into other points of stylistic correspondence.” This shows that Text 2 views form-based scholarship as a restriction—it prevents exploration of broader stylistic elements that could deepen understanding of modernism.

steps.title: Apply this diagnosis to the choices
steps.content: Answer C—”forestalling consideration of the full stylistic dimensions”—exactly captures what Text 2 identifies as the problem: Text 1's form-emphasis prevents the fuller stylistic inquiry that would deepen modernist scholarship. The other answers either misrepresent Text 2's concern, overstate to misconception, or wrongly suggest form-study aids broader inquiry. This matches choice C — C is correct.
